changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: SIGNAL: Iran easing Hormuz restrictions per maritime data — countries cutting individual deals instead of joining US coalition. This is a partial de-escalation on the economic front, though military strikes continue. Europe refuses Hormuz naval deployment. Putin-Trump call today is the key diplomatic variable for Ukraine. Zero ceasefire signals in ME active theaters.

latest_news.headline: IRGC orders evacuation of Gulf energy assets (Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE)
latest_news.text: IRGC issues evacuation order for all energy assets in Qatar, Saudi Arabia and UAE. 'These facilities have become direct and legitimate targets and will be targeted in the coming hours.' Citizens and employees ordered to leave immediately.

latest_news.headline: IRGC threatens retaliation for South Pars — 'will strongly attack the source of aggression'
latest_news.text: Khatam al-Anbiya spokesman says Tehran will 'strongly attack the source of aggression.' Iran Ministry of Oil confirms Asaluyeh facilities damaged, cooling and fire operations underway. No casualties reported.

latest_news.headline: Oil approaches $110/barrel on South Pars strike + Gulf escalation
latest_news.text: Global benchmark crude oil approaches $110/barrel following South Pars strikes and IRGC Gulf evacuation orders. Natural gas prices also spiked.

latest_news.headline: IDF to attack Litani River crossings — mass evacuation order
latest_news.text: IDF announces it will attack bridges across Litani River in southern Lebanon starting noon Wednesday. Orders所有人 south of Litani to evacuate north immediately. Moving southward 'may endanger your lives.'

outlook: CRITICAL (Day 24, 16:04 UTC) — ESCALATING: (1) IRGC orders evacuation of ALL Gulf energy assets — Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE called 'legitimate targets'; (2) IRGC vows retaliation for South Pars strikes; (3) Oil ~$110/barrel; (4) IDF to strike Litani River crossings; (5) Asaluyeh facilities damaged confirmed. This is the most dangerous moment — Gulf energy infrastructure explicitly threatened by IRGC. Iran moving from strategic restraint to targeting Gulf economies directly. If Gulf assets struck, conflict becomes fully regional.
